objective: Convert collected shadow results into a safe policy for deciding when qualification-aware routing is allowed to take control, with evidence aggregation and per-track enforcement states.

results: 60/60 enforcement-policy tests pass (default state, set state, states enum, override CRUD, approve track, policy summary, evidence review, evidence review empty, per-track review, 10 eligibility conditions)
Track run record schema tests pass (4/4, including schema with shadowRecommendation)
Zero companion imports from benchmark-lab/engine/ — architectural boundary preserved
All enforcement is off by default (all tracks in shadow mode)

passed: companion/core/enforcement-policy.js created — defines 5 rollout states (disabled, shadow, eligible, enforced, suspended) with per-track granularity

Enforcement eligibility evaluated across 8+ conditions: track state, qualification state, score threshold, active overrides, runtime availability, model readiness, track approval, comparison state validity
companion/evidence/shadow-evidence-review.js created — aggregates shadow routing comparisons from Track Run Records
Evidence review computes: total comparisons, agreement rate, disagreement rate, current-selection-unqualified count, coverage rate, by-track breakdowns
GET /enforcement/status — returns policy summary + evidence review
POST /enforcement/approve — approves a track for enforcement
GET /enforcement/review — returns detailed review with optional trackId filter
GET /enforcement/eligibility — checks if a recommendation is eligible for enforcement
Default state for all tracks is 'shadow'
Enforcement never enabled by default — off by design
60 tests in scripts/test-enforcement-policy.js cover all required scenarios
